adaptation.description: Make edition number visually dominant on EditionCard shop grid cards
adaptation.hypothesis: A larger, separated 'no. X' provenance mark at 16px/black increases collector engagement vs the compact 'just N made · no. X is next' single line
adaptation.change: EditionMarkLine split into two lines: 'no. X' at text-[16px] font-black in accent color (was xs extrabold inline), 'just N made' at text-[11px] font-extrabold text-muted below
adaptation.direction: expected positive — provenance prominence is a documented conversion driver in numbered-edition collector UX

delta_vs_baseline.note: delta is exactly 0 — expected behavior; cohort engine is deterministic (seed=42) and engine-blind to UI surface changes; identical results confirm engine correctness, not that UI change had no effect

non_claims: SYNTHETIC-ONLY: these KPIs do not represent real collector behavior or real commerce outcomes
does not prove real digital ownership or asset transfer
does not involve any blockchain, wallet, or token — ever
synthetic collectibles only — no financial value, no resale
zero-delta between baseline and iteration is a property of the synthetic engine, not a finding about UI efficacy
the hypothesis (provenance prominence drives engagement) is untested in real user data — this sprint does not claim otherwise
